linux-gfx90a.blocked_reason: Scoped libcudf core now COMPILES at 83 TUs for gfx90a (real gfx90a code object) and a focused GPU gtest passes 6/6 on MI250X, stable over 20 repeated runs (4 wave64 null-mask tests + 2 new tuple-key radix-sort tests). This session CLEARED the 3 prior 'next walls': (1) rocPRIM radix sort over cuda::std::tuple keys is SOLVED by a reusable, rocPRIM-unmodified decomposer-adaptor shim (cpp/include/cudf/detail/utilities/hip/radix_tuple_key.cuh) that re-ties the decomposer's references into a rocprim::tuple so rocPRIM's existing radix codec drives the sort -- liftable verbatim into cuco retrieve_all and raft; GPU-proven (sort_radix/sorted_order_radix FLOAT32 asc+desc == host std::sort, sizes 1..100000); sorted_order_radix also needed its zip-iterator functor to return thrust::tuple not cuda::std::pair (rocThrust). (2) cuda::std::array<uint32_t,100> in strings/case.cu was NOT a real wall -- the type is supported by the vendored libhipcxx; case.cu just lacked an explicit #include <cuda/std/array> (added). (3) the wave64 ballot in strings/copying/concatenate.cu converted to the tile-relative ballot_32 helper. Remaining to a FULLY-linking libcudf + broad GPU suite: the rest of the type-erased dispatch link-closure (~209 deferred-dispatch undefined symbols in the .so -- the broad cuco-backed algorithm modules: join/groupby/hashing/reductions/distinct + full strings/lists/dictionary/structs algorithm sets), then the jitify->hipRTC JIT phase (plan written, PoC proven). src/io (nvCOMP/KvikIO) stays scoped out. Honest state stays PORTING. See notes.md.
